news.titleEn: Iran begins mass funeral procession in Tehran for slain Supreme Leader Khamenei
news.conclusionEn: Iran began a nationwide funeral procession for Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, killed in a February airstrike, with officials expecting 15 to 20 million mourners.
news.conclusionZh: 伊朗为2月遇袭身亡的最高领袖哈梅内伊举行国葬，预计吸引1500万至2000万人悼念。
news.whyEn: Crowds are chanting for revenge against the US and Israel, raising fresh fears of renewed escalation in the region just as Washington pushes for an Iran peace deal.
news.whyZh: 民众高喊复仇，令外界担忧地区局势再升级，恰逢美方正推动伊朗和谈。
news.detailEn: Khamenei's flag-draped coffin, along with those of family members killed in the same February strike, was paraded through Tehran on Monday as hundreds of thousands lined the streets chanting "death to America." Officials say the ceremonies could draw between 15 and 20 million mourners nationwide, potentially making it the largest state funeral in Iran's history. The identity of Iran's next supreme leader has not been announced, leaving the country's future leadership and its approach to ongoing talks with Washington uncertain.
news.detailZh: 哈梅内伊的灵柩7月6日周一被送上卡车游行穿过德黑兰街头，与他一同遇难的家属灵柩也一并送行，数十万民众沿街高喊「打倒美国」。官方预计全国悼念人数将达1500万至2000万，可能成为伊朗历史上规模最大的国葬。伊朗尚未公布新任最高领袖人选，未来领导层归属及对美谈判走向仍充满不确定性。

news.titleEn: NATO leaders look for unity as Trump attends annual summit in Ankara
news.conclusionEn: NATO's 32 leaders meet in Ankara on July 7-8 with Trump attending amid friction over defense spending, and allies are set to unveil a $75 billion Ukraine aid package.
news.conclusionZh: 北约峰会7日在安卡拉召开，特朗普与会，盟国拟推750亿美元援乌方案。
news.whyEn: Trump is pushing allies toward 5% of GDP defense spending and demanding "loyalty," testing alliance cohesion at a moment of heightened Middle East tension.
news.whyZh: 特朗普要求盟国将防务开支提至GDP的5%并强调「忠诚」，考验北约团结。
news.detailEn: The 36th NATO summit opens Tuesday in Ankara, the first hosted by Turkey since 2004, with Secretary General Mark Rutte prioritizing higher defense investment, defense-industrial capacity, and continued support for Ukraine. Trump has said he considered skipping the summit but is attending partly out of goodwill toward Turkish President Erdogan, while publicly pressing members to raise defense spending to 5% of GDP. Allies are expected to announce a landmark $75 billion military and financial aid package for Ukraine, easing pressure on the US to fund Kyiv alone. Turkey is separately seeking US approval to buy F-110 engines and rejoin the F-35 program.
news.detailZh: 第36届北约峰会7日在土耳其安卡拉开幕，这是继2004年伊斯坦布尔峰会后土耳其再度承办。秘书长吕特将峰会重点放在提高防务投入、扩大军工产能及持续援乌上。特朗普曾表示考虑缺席，但为给土耳其总统埃尔多安面子而与会，同时公开要求成员国将防务开支提高到GDP的5%。盟国预计将宣布一项750亿美元的对乌军事及财政援助方案，以减轻美国独自承担援乌压力。土耳其则另外寻求美方批准其采购F-110发动机并重返F-35战机项目。

news.titleEn: Trump says Netanyahu "knows who the boss is" ahead of expected White House visit
news.conclusionEn: Trump and Netanyahu remain at odds over Israel's offensive in Lebanon even as a White House meeting is being arranged, with Trump publicly asserting control over Israeli policy.
news.conclusionZh: 特朗普与内塔尼亚胡就黎巴嫩军事行动分歧未消，白宫会晤仍在筹备中。
news.whyEn: Rare public friction between the US and its closest Middle East ally could shape whether Israel scales back operations in Lebanon as Iran-related diplomacy continues.
news.whyZh: 美以罕见公开分歧，可能影响以色列黎巴嫩行动走向及伊朗相关外交进程。
news.detailEn: Trump has pressed Netanyahu to restrain Israeli military operations in Lebanon, which Washington sees as an obstacle to broader efforts to end hostilities with Iran, after a heated phone call last month in which Trump reportedly called Netanyahu's approach reckless. A White House visit by Netanyahu is being discussed but is unlikely to happen immediately since Trump is traveling to the NATO summit in Turkey this week. Despite the tension, Trump told reporters "we get along very good" and that Netanyahu "knows who the boss is," underscoring Washington's push to keep Israel aligned with US regional strategy.
news.detailZh: 特朗普上月与内塔尼亚胡通话时言辞激烈，据报道批评其黎巴嫩行动方式过于冒进，此后一直施压要求以方克制军事行动，因为华盛顿认为这是推进伊朗停火进程的障碍。内塔尼亚胡访问白宫一事仍在磋商中，但由于特朗普本周将赴土耳其出席北约峰会，会晤短期内难以成行。尽管存在分歧，特朗普仍对记者表示「我们关系很好」，并称内塔尼亚胡「知道谁说了算」，凸显美方希望以色列继续配合其中东战略部署。

news.titleEn: Google Loses EU Court Appeal Over €4.1 Billion Android Antitrust Fine
news.conclusionEn: The EU's top court finalized a record €4.1 billion ($4.7 billion) antitrust fine against Google over Android, ending the company's last avenue of appeal.
news.conclusionZh: 欧盟最高法院终审维持对谷歌41亿欧元反垄断罚款，谷歌已无上诉余地。
news.whyEn: The ruling cements the EU's toughest-ever tech antitrust penalty and reinforces regulators' power to police how dominant platforms bundle their own services.
news.whyZh: 此案是欧盟史上最重的科技反垄断罚单，强化了监管机构限制平台捆绑行为的权力。
news.detailEn: The European Court of Justice ruled Thursday that the European Commission was right to punish Google for pressuring Android phone makers to preinstall Google Search and Chrome, effectively shutting out rivals. The original 2018 fine of €4.34 billion was reduced to €4.1 billion by a lower EU court in 2022, and Thursday's ruling makes that reduced figure final with no further right of appeal. The decision closes an eight-year legal battle and signals that the EU's tightening scrutiny of Big Tech's platform power will continue under the newer Digital Markets Act regime.
news.detailZh: 欧盟法院周四裁定，欧盟委员会此前因谷歌向安卓手机厂商施压、要求预装谷歌搜索和Chrome浏览器、从而排挤竞争对手的行为进行处罚是正确的。这笔罚款原为2018年开出的43.4亿欧元，2022年被欧盟下级法院减至41亿欧元，此次终审维持该金额且谷歌已无法继续上诉。这场长达八年的法律拉锯战就此落幕，也表明欧盟在《数字市场法》框架下对大型科技平台的监管力度仍将持续收紧。

news.titleEn: Meta Is Planning a Cloud Business to Sell AI Computing Power
news.conclusionEn: Meta is building a new cloud unit, internally called "Meta Compute," to sell its excess AI computing capacity to outside customers, competing with AWS, Azure and Google Cloud.
news.conclusionZh: Meta拟组建云业务「Meta Compute」，对外出售闲置AI算力，进军云计算市场。
news.whyEn: With roughly $145 billion in 2026 infrastructure spending, monetizing spare AI capacity could turn Meta's massive data-center buildout into a new revenue stream.
news.whyZh: Meta今年基建投入约1450亿美元，出售闲置算力有望开辟新的收入来源。
news.detailEn: The initiative is overseen by infrastructure chief Santosh Janardhan, Meta Superintelligence Labs' Daniel Gross, and Meta President Dina Powell McCormick, and will offer two products: hosted access to Meta's AI models, and raw GPU compute capacity sold directly to enterprise customers. Analysts estimate Meta's total infrastructure spending will reach about $145 billion in 2026, and the new unit aims to generate revenue from that build-out rather than let capacity sit idle. The move puts Meta in direct competition with established cloud providers and mirrors similar plays by other AI-heavy companies looking to monetize surplus compute.
news.detailZh: 该项目由Meta基础设施负责人Santosh Janardhan、Meta超级智能实验室的Daniel Gross及总裁Dina Powell McCormick共同负责，将推出两类产品：一是让企业调用Meta AI模型的托管服务，二是直接出售原始GPU算力。分析师预计Meta今年基础设施总支出将达约1450亿美元，新业务旨在为这些巨额投入创造收入，而非让算力闲置。此举使Meta与现有云服务巨头正面竞争，也与其他AI企业近期纷纷变现闲置算力的做法如出一辙。

news.titleEn: June jobs report: US payrolls rose by 57,000, missing expectations
news.conclusionEn: US nonfarm payrolls grew just 57,000 in June, far below the 115,000 forecast, though unemployment ticked down to 4.2%.
news.conclusionZh: 美国6月新增就业仅5.7万人，远低于预期，失业率微降至4.2%。
news.whyEn: The weak print, plus large downward revisions to April and May, adds to signs of a cooling labor market that could influence the Fed's next rate decision.
news.whyZh: 就业数据疲软且前两月被大幅下修，凸显劳动力市场降温，或影响美联储下次决策。
news.detailEn: The Bureau of Labor Statistics said professional and business services, social assistance, and health care led job gains, while leisure and hospitality shed 61,000 positions on weaker seasonal hiring. April's payroll count was revised down by 31,000 and May's by 43,000, leaving the two months a combined 74,000 jobs weaker than first reported. Average hourly earnings rose 0.3% to $37.64, keeping annual wage growth at 3.5%. The soft report comes ahead of the Fed's July 28-29 meeting, where officials have already removed a 2026 rate cut from their projections.
news.detailZh: 美国劳工统计局数据显示，专业和商业服务、社会救助及医疗保健行业带动了就业增长，而休闲酒店业因季节性用工走弱减少6.1万个岗位。4月就业人数被下修3.1万，5月被下修4.3万，两月合计较此前公布数据少增7.4万个岗位。平均时薪上涨0.3%至37.64美元，同比薪资增速维持在3.5%。这份疲软报告发布之际，美联储将于7月28至29日召开会议，此前官员已从预测中撤下2026年降息的可能。

news.titleEn: OPEC+ approves further oil output increase as Hormuz exports start to recover
news.conclusionEn: OPEC+ agreed to raise output quotas by 188,000 barrels per day from August as flows through the Strait of Hormuz recover, pushing crude prices near $68-69 a barrel.
news.conclusionZh: OPEC+决定8月起日增产18.8万桶，霍尔木兹海峡出口恢复致油价承压。
news.whyEn: Falling oil prices ease inflation pressure for consumers but squeeze producer revenues, and signal easing Middle East supply risk after the Iran war.
news.whyZh: 油价下行利好消费者通胀压力，但冲击产油国收入，也显示中东供应风险正在缓解。
news.detailEn: The core OPEC+ members, including Russia, have now raised output quotas by nearly 800,000 barrels per day since April as the UAE restored exports above 3.9 million barrels per day and Saudi Arabia ramped up shipments to Asia. Combined flows through the Strait of Hormuz have climbed back above 10 million barrels a day, reversing the disruption caused by the Iran war earlier this year. Crude has fallen to around $68-69 a barrel, its lowest since before the conflict erupted in late February, as traders weigh the risk of a supply glut against still-firm demand.
news.detailZh: 以俄罗斯为代表的OPEC+核心成员国自4月以来已累计增产近80万桶/日，阿联酋出口量恢复至390万桶/日以上，沙特也加大对亚洲的出口。经霍尔木兹海峡的合计原油流量已回升至每日1000万桶以上，扭转了今年早些时候伊朗战争造成的供应中断局面。国际油价已跌至2月冲突爆发前的水平，约每桶68至69美元，交易员正在权衡供应过剩风险与仍然稳健的需求前景。

news.titleEn: Apple to introduce at least five new iPhone models amid memory shortages: Report
news.conclusionEn: Apple reportedly plans to launch at least five new iPhone models through early 2027, headlined by its first foldable device priced around $2,500.
news.conclusionZh: 苹果据报明年前将推出至少五款新iPhone，首款折叠屏定价约2500美元。
news.whyEn: The aggressive lineup expansion, amid a global memory chip shortage, signals Apple's bid to grab market share from Android rivals even as component costs rise.
news.whyZh: 在全球存储芯片短缺背景下加速推新，显示苹果欲抢占安卓阵营市场份额。
news.detailEn: According to Nikkei Asia, Apple has told suppliers to prepare roughly 10 million foldable iPhones for this year, up from an earlier estimate of 7-8 million, ahead of the device's expected launch. The company has already secured components for around 80 million smartphones for the second half of 2026, with total 2026 production expected to exceed 220 million units. The push comes as a global memory shortage pushes up component prices, which could raise costs for consumers, but Apple appears to be betting that a broader model lineup, including its first foldable, will offset any pricing pressure.
news.detailZh: 据《日经亚洲》报道，苹果已要求供应商为今年准备约1000万部折叠屏iPhone，高于此前700万至800万部的预估，为该机型上市做准备。公司已为2026年下半年约8000万部智能手机备齐零部件，预计全年总产量将超过2.2亿部。此举是在全球存储芯片短缺推高零部件价格的背景下推进的，消费者成本可能因此上升，但苹果似乎押注更丰富的产品线（包括首款折叠屏机型）能够抵消定价压力。

news.titleEn: Global Startup Investment Hit Record $510B in H1 2026 as AI Boom Accelerates Funding and Exits
news.conclusionEn: Global venture funding hit a record $510 billion in H1 2026, with OpenAI and Anthropic alone accounting for 43% of the total as AI dominates capital allocation.
news.conclusionZh: 全球创投融资上半年创510亿美元纪录，OpenAI与Anthropic占比高达43%。
news.whyEn: The extreme concentration of capital in a handful of AI labs shows how venture investing has narrowed, with implications for founders outside the frontier-AI race.
news.whyZh: 资本高度集中于少数AI公司，反映创投格局收窄，对非前沿AI创业者影响深远。
news.detailEn: Investors poured $305 billion into startups in Q1 and $205 billion in Q2, surpassing the previous half-year record of $375 billion set in H2 2021 and already exceeding all of 2025's full-year total of $440 billion. AI-focused companies captured more than 70% of all global startup capital in Q2, up from roughly 50% a year earlier. The period also produced the strongest exit market since 2021, including SpaceX's public listing at a $1.77 trillion valuation raising $75 billion, and Anysphere, maker of AI coding tool Cursor, being acquired for $60 billion.
news.detailZh: 今年一季度创投资金流入达3050亿美元，二季度再增2050亿美元，超过2021年下半年3750亿美元的历史同期纪录，也已超过2025年全年4400亿美元的总额。二季度AI相关企业获得的全球创投资金占比超过70%，较一年前的约50%大幅提升。这一时期也出现了2021年以来最强劲的退出行情，包括SpaceX以1.77万亿美元估值上市并募资750亿美元，以及AI编程工具Cursor的开发商Anysphere被以600亿美元收购。

news.titleEn: Pastor freed from prison in China weeks after Trump requested his release
news.conclusionEn: Chinese pastor Ezra Jin Mingri was released and reunited with family in Los Angeles, less than two months after Trump raised his case directly with Xi Jinping.
news.conclusionZh: 中国牧师金明日获释并抵达洛杉矶，此前特朗普曾向习近平提及其案件。
news.whyEn: The release highlights how high-level US-China diplomacy can affect individual human rights cases, even as most detained church members remain jailed.
news.whyZh: 此事显示中美高层外交可影响个案人权处境，但多数被捕教会成员仍在押。
news.detailEn: Jin, a leader of the underground Zion Church, was among 18 church leaders detained in October in one of China's largest crackdowns on a single congregation in decades. His case drew attention after Trump said he raised both Jin's detention and that of jailed Hong Kong activist Jimmy Lai while returning from a state visit to Beijing in May. Jin has since arrived in Los Angeles and reunited with his family, but rights advocates note that most of the other detained Zion Church leaders remain in custody facing serious criminal charges.
news.detailZh: 金明日是地下教会「锡安教会」的负责人，去年10月与另外17名教会领袖一同被捕，是中国近几十年来针对单一教会规模最大的打压行动之一。他的案件受到关注，是因为特朗普今年5月结束对北京的国事访问返程途中曾表示，已就金明日及被关押的香港活动人士黎智英案向习近平提出交涉。金明日目前已抵达洛杉矶与家人团聚，但人权组织指出，锡安教会其他大多数被捕领袖仍被羁押，面临严重刑事指控。

businessTips.explanationEn: The sunk cost fallacy is the tendency to keep investing time or money into a decision because of what's already been spent, rather than evaluating it based on future value alone. Sunk costs — money, time, or effort already spent — are gone regardless of what you do next, so rational decisions should ignore them entirely. Good managers train themselves to ask "knowing what I know now, would I start this today?" instead of "how much have we already put in?" This mindset prevents organizations from throwing good resources after bad.
businessTips.explanationZh: 沉没成本谬误指的是因为已经投入了时间或金钱，就继续投入某个决策，而不是单纯根据未来价值来判断是否值得继续。已经花掉的成本无论后续怎么做都无法收回，所以理性决策应该完全忽略它们。优秀的管理者会训练自己问「如果现在从零开始，我还会做这个选择吗」，而不是「我们已经投入了多少」。这种思维能避免组织在错误的方向上继续追加资源。
businessTips.exampleEn: A company has spent $2 million building a product feature that user testing shows nobody wants. The sunk cost fallacy would push the team to finish it anyway to "justify" the spend, while sound decision-making means killing the feature now and redirecting the remaining budget to something with real demand.
businessTips.exampleZh: 某公司已投入200万美元开发一项功能，但用户测试显示几乎无人需要。若陷入沉没成本谬误，团队可能会为「对得起已花的钱」而硬着头皮做完；而正确的做法是立即砍掉该功能，把剩余预算投入真正有需求的方向。

businessTips.explanationEn: A productized service packages your skills or custom work into a fixed-scope, fixed-price offering — like a menu item — instead of open-ended hourly consulting. This makes the service easier to market, sell, and deliver repeatedly, because clients know exactly what they're getting and you know exactly how much time it takes. It's a popular side-hustle model because it lets people with a specialized skill (design, writing, code audits, bookkeeping) scale beyond trading hours for dollars without needing to build a full product from scratch.
businessTips.explanationZh: 产品化服务是把你的技能或定制化工作，打包成一个范围固定、价格固定的「套餐」，而不是按小时无限期地做咨询。这样客户清楚自己能得到什么，你也清楚每单大概要花多少时间，服务更容易被推广、销售和重复交付。这是很受欢迎的副业模式，让拥有专业技能（如设计、写作、代码审查、记账）的人不必从零打造完整产品，也能突破「用时间换钱」的瓶颈实现规模化。
businessTips.exampleEn: Instead of offering open-ended freelance graphic design at an hourly rate, a designer creates a fixed package: "5 social media posts in 3 days for $300." Clients can buy it instantly without negotiating scope, and the designer can deliver several packages per week using a repeatable process.
businessTips.exampleZh: 一位设计师不再按小时接无边界的自由设计项目，而是推出固定套餐：「3天内交付5张社媒配图，300美元」。客户无需就工作范围反复沟通即可直接下单，设计师也能借助标准化流程，一周内交付多个这样的套餐订单。

businessTips.explanationEn: An economic moat is a company's durable competitive advantage that protects its profits from competitors over the long run, a term popularized by Warren Buffett. Common sources of a moat include network effects, high switching costs, brand power, patents or regulatory licenses, and cost advantages from scale. Investors look for moats because a company that can defend its margins for a decade or more tends to compound shareholder value more reliably than one facing constant price competition.
businessTips.explanationZh: 经济护城河是指一家公司能长期保护自身利润、抵御竞争对手侵蚀的可持续竞争优势，这一概念由巴菲特推广而广为人知。常见的护城河来源包括网络效应、高转换成本、品牌力、专利或牌照壁垒，以及规模带来的成本优势。投资者重视护城河，是因为能在十年甚至更长时间里守住利润率的公司，往往比长期陷入价格战的公司更能持续为股东创造复利式增长。
businessTips.exampleEn: Google's dominance in search and Android, the very behavior that just triggered its €4.1 billion EU antitrust fine, illustrates a moat built on network effects and default-setting power on billions of devices — hard for rivals to replicate even under regulatory pressure. Investors weigh whether such a moat can survive years of antitrust action before deciding if it's still intact.
businessTips.exampleZh: 谷歌在搜索和安卓系统上的主导地位——也正是导致其被欧盟处以41亿欧元反垄断罚款的行为——体现了一种建立在网络效应和数十亿设备默认设置权之上的护城河，即便面临监管压力，竞争对手也难以复制。投资者需要判断，这样的护城河能否在多年反垄断诉讼后依然坚固。
